15.reason: [The comparison function for qsort must return negative, zero, or positive values consistently. This implementation returns -1 when e1 > e2 and 1 otherwise, which breaks strict weak ordering (e.g., when e1 == e2, it returns 1 instead of 0), leading to undefined behavior in qsort.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [The comparison function for qsort must return negative, zero, or positive values consistently. This implementation returns -1 when e1 > e2 and 1 otherwise, which breaks strict weak ordering (e.g., when e1 == e2, it returns 1 instead of 0), leading to undefined behavior in qsort.]

22.reason: [If "article.txt" does not exist or cannot be opened, fopen returns NULL, and subsequent fgetc(fp) causes undefined behavior. The program should check if fp is NULL and handle the error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [If "article.txt" does not exist or cannot be opened, fopen returns NULL, and subsequent fgetc(fp) causes undefined behavior. The program should check if fp is NULL and handle the error.]

28.reason: [This conversion is incorrect: 'a' - 'A' is positive, but adding it to an uppercase letter gives wrong result. Should be c = c - 'A' + 'a'; or simply use tolower(c).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [This conversion is incorrect: 'a' - 'A' is positive, but adding it to an uppercase letter gives wrong result. Should be c = c - 'A' + 'a'; or simply use tolower(c).]

37.reason: [When flag != 0, j is not reset, but ++j increments before assignment, potentially skipping index 0 and causing out-of-bounds if j exceeds 499. Also, no null-termination of word[i].s after reading a word.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [When flag != 0, j is not reset, but ++j increments before assignment, potentially skipping index 0 and causing out-of-bounds if j exceeds 499. Also, no null-termination of word[i].s after reading a word.]

44.reason: [After encountering a non-alpha character, j is reset to 0, but the current word string is not null-terminated, so strcmp later may read garbage.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [After encountering a non-alpha character, j is reset to 0, but the current word string is not null-terminated, so strcmp later may read garbage.]

58.reason: [Overwriting only first 3 characters with 'z' doesn't reliably invalidate the string; also, if original word is longer than 3 chars, strcmp may still match partially. Should set word[p].s[0] = '\0' or mark differently.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [Overwriting only first 3 characters with 'z' doesn't reliably invalidate the string; also, if original word is longer than 3 chars, strcmp may still match partially. Should set word[p].s[0] = '\0' or mark differently.]

66.reason: [Due to incorrect cmp function and possible unterminated strings, qsort behavior is undefined. Also, i-out+1 may be incorrect because out counts duplicates but doesn't account for multiple duplicates properly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [Due to incorrect cmp function and possible unterminated strings, qsort behavior is undefined. Also, i-out+1 may be incorrect because out counts duplicates but doesn't account for multiple duplicates properly.]

69.reason: [If any word string was not null-terminated during input, printf may print garbage beyond intended word.]
